rationale: The World Bank warning that a US-Iran war could drag the global economy to post-COVID lows is the most relevant new signal for Poland, reinforcing existing energy price and economic stability concerns; all other headlines are continuations or geographically distant.

top_events.title: World Bank: US-Iran war to pull global economy to post-COVID low
top_events.summary: World Bank warns that an escalating US-Iran conflict could cause the worst global economic downturn since COVID-19, directly threatening EU and Polish economic stability and energy prices.

top_events.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17
top_events.summary: Ongoing Israeli strikes in Lebanon continue; no qualitative escalation beyond established background conflict entangled with Iran situation.

top_events.title: Xi Jinping visits Pyongyang to revitalise China-North Korea ties
top_events.summary: China deepening ties with North Korea could shift geopolitical alignments relevant to NATO's eastern flank, though impact on Poland remains indirect and uncertain.
